App Bootstrapping **Entry Point: `index.html`** (Lines 1-20) ```html T3 Code (Alpha)

Context/Providers Pattern **Provider Architecture:** 1. **QueryClientProvider** (`@tanstack/react-query`) - Manages server state, API queries - Created in `src/router.ts` (lines 10-25) 2. **StoreProvider** (Custom Zustand provider) - Wraps app in router (line 23 in `router.ts`) - Simple pass-through that ensures persisted state is hydrated on mount 3. **ToastProvider & AnchoredToastProvider** (Custom) - Manages toast notifications - Located in `src/components/ui/toast.tsx` - From `__root.tsx` lines 50-56 4. **DiffWorkerPoolProvider** (Custom) - Manages worker pool for diff rendering - Located in `src/components/DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx` - Wraps chat routes with Web Workers for diff processing - Syncs theme with worker pool (lines 7-29) 5. **SidebarProvider** (from `@base-ui/react`) - Manages sidebar open/closed state - Used in `_chat.tsx` line 28 6. **Theme Hook** (`useTheme` in `src/hooks/useTheme.ts`) - Not a provider, but manages theme globally via `useSyncExternalStore` - Stores theme preference in localStorage --- ### 4. Custom Hooks Located in `/apps/web/src/hooks/`: 1. **`useTheme.ts`** (Lines 1-105) - Returns: `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }` - Uses `useSyncExternalStore` for external theme state - Applies theme to `document.documentElement.classList` ("dark" class) - Listens to system preference changes via media queries - Handles multi-tab sync via storage events 2. **`useMediaQuery.ts`** (Lines 1-27) - Tracks CSS media query matches - Example: `useMediaQuery("(max-width: 1180px)")` - Used for responsive layouts 3. **`useTurnDiffSummaries.ts`** - Custom hook for turn diff data management --- ### 5. TypeScript Configuration **`tsconfig.json`** (app-specific): ```json { "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json", "compilerOptions": { "jsx": "react-jsx", "lib": ["ES2023", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"], "paths": { "~/*": ["./src/*"] // Path alias for imports }, "plugins": [ { "name": "@effect/language-service", "diagnosticSeverity": { /* ... */ } } ] } } ``` **Key Points:** - Extends monorepo base config (`tsconfig.base.json`) - `jsx: "react-jsx"` for automatic JSX transform - Path alias `~/*` for cleaner imports: `import { Button } from "~/components/ui/button"` - Effect language service plugin for better type checking - `composite: true` for Turbo monorepo support --- ### 7. Local Storage Usage **Storage Keys (by feature):** 1. **Theme** (`useTheme.ts` line 9) - Key: `"t3code:theme"` - Stores: `"light" | "dark" | "system"` - Persisted globally, synced across tabs 2. **App State** (`store.ts` lines 26-37) - Key: `"t3code:renderer-state:v8"` - Stores: Expanded project directories for UI state - Auto-persists on state changes (line 399) - Legacy keys cleaned up on migration 3. **Composer Drafts** (`composerDraftStore.ts` line 20) - Key: `"t3code:composer-drafts:v1"` - Stores: User's draft prompts with attachments per thread - Uses Zustand's `persist` middleware with `createJSONStorage` - Survives page refreshes 4. **Terminal State** (`terminalStateStore.ts` line 28) - Key: `"t3code:terminal-state:v1"` - Stores: Terminal UI state (open, height, active terminal ID) - Per-thread terminal configuration 5. **App Settings** (`appSettings.ts` line 6) - Key: `"t3code:app-settings:v1"` - Stores: User preferences (Codex binary path, model selection, service tier) - Uses Effect schema validation - Enforced max field lengths (4096 chars for paths) 6. **Sidebar State** (`components/ui/sidebar.tsx`) - Sidebar open/closed state stored per route **Storage Strategy:** - Zustand `persist` middleware with JSON serialization - Manual localStorage access for theme (custom external store pattern) - Graceful error handling: quota errors logged but don't break UX - Version management with legacy key cleanup --- ### 8. (other components) │ │ │ ├── hooks/ # Custom React hooks │ │ ├── useTheme.ts # Theme management │ │ ├── useMediaQuery.ts # CSS media queries │ │ └── useTurnDiffSummaries.ts │ │ │ ├── lib/ # Utilities & helpers │ │ ├── utils.ts # cn() for Tailwind + misc helpers │ │ ├── gitReactQuery.ts # Git mutation/query options │ │ ├── providerReactQuery.ts # Provider queries │ │ ├── projectReactQuery.ts # Project queries │ │ ├── serverReactQuery.ts # Server config queries │ │ ├── diffRendering.ts # Diff theme resolver │ │ ├── lruCache.ts # LRU cache utility │ │ └── ... (other utilities & features) │ ├── dist/ # Build output (ignored) └── node_modules/ # Dependencies (ignored) ``` --- ### 9. Key Dependencies **Core Framework:** - `react@19.0.0` - UI library - `react-dom@19.0.0` - DOM rendering - `@tanstack/react-router@1.160.2` - File-based routing - `@tanstack/react-query@5.90.0` - Server state management **UI & Styling:** - `tailwindcss@4.0.0` - Utility-first CSS - `@tailwindcss/vite@4.0.0` - Vite plugin for Tailwind - `@base-ui/react@1.2.0` - Headless UI components - `lucide-react@0.564.0` - Icon library - `class-variance-authority@0.7.1` - Component variants **State Management:** - `zustand@5.0.11` - Light state management store - `effect@catalog:` - Effect functional programming library **Rich Text & Markdown:** - `@lexical/react@0.41.0` - Rich text editor framework - `react-markdown@10.1.0` - Markdown to React rendering - `remark-gfm@4.0.1` - GitHub-flavored markdown **Terminal:** - `@xterm/xterm@6.0.0` - Terminal emulator - `@xterm/addon-fit@0.11.0` - Terminal fit addon **Diff Rendering:** - `@pierre/diffs@1.1.0-beta.16` - Code diff rendering with Web Workers **Build Tools:** - `vite@8.0.0-beta.12` - Build tool - `@vitejs/plugin-react@5.1.4` - React plugin - `@tanstack/router-plugin@1.161.0` - Router plugin - `babel-plugin-react-compiler@19.0.0-beta` - React compiler optimization **Testing:** - `vitest` - Unit test runner - `@vitest/browser-playwright` - Browser testing - `msw@2.12.10` - API mocking --- ### 10. # Custom Theme System — Implementation Plan ## Context T3 Code currently has a minimal light/dark/system mode toggle. The `useTheme` hook in `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` stores one of `"light" | "dark" | "system"` in localStorage, toggles a `.dark` CSS class on ``, and returns `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }`. Everything else is driven by Tailwind CSS v4 custom properties in `src/index.css` (`:root` with `@variant dark` block). The goal is to replace this minimal system with a named-theme architecture that defines structured color palettes (background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, statusBar) plus fontFamily/fontSize, ships 3 built-in themes, exposes a `useTheme()` hook with `availableThemes`, and adds a visual ThemeSelector component wired into the existing Settings page Appearance section. --- ## Critical Files | Path | Role | |---|---| | `packages/shared/package.json` | Add `./themes` subpath export | | `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` | NEW — Theme interface + built-ins + helpers | | `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | OVERHAUL — multi-theme hook | | `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | NEW — visual theme picker | | `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | MODIFY — swap Appearance section | | `apps/web/src/index.css` | MODIFY — add `--theme-font-family` var to body | No build-config changes, no new dependencies. --- ## Step-by-Step Implementation ### Step 1 — `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` (new file) Pure data module, no DOM/runtime deps. Follow the same style as `git.ts`/`model.ts`: JSDoc, `readonly` properties, `as const satisfies`, exported pure functions. ```typescript export interface ThemeColors { readonly background: string; // main app background readonly foreground: string; // primary text readonly accent: string; // primary action color (buttons, focus rings) readonly sidebar: string; // sidebar panel background readonly editor: string; // editor pane background readonly terminal: string; // terminal background readonly statusBar: string; // status-bar background } export interface Theme { readonly id: string; readonly name: string; readonly colors: ThemeColors; readonly fontFamily: string; readonly fontSize: number; // base font size in px readonly isDark: boolean; // drives .dark class toggle for Tailwind } ``` Three built-in themes (colors chosen to match / diverge from existing CSS): - **`"t3-dark"`** (default): matches the existing dark-mode CSS variables (`oklch(0.14 0 0)` bg, `oklch(0.96 0 0)` fg, `oklch(0.588 0.217 264)` accent). - **`"t3-light"`**: matches the existing light-mode CSS variables (`#ffffff` bg, `oklch(0.271 0 0)` fg, `oklch(0.488 0.217 264)` accent). - **`"t3-midnight"`**: deep blue-tinted dark; distinct from t3-dark (`oklch(0.1 0.015 265)` bg, `oklch(0.93 0.01 265)` fg, `oklch(0.65 0.26 275)` accent). Export: ```typescript export const DEFAULT_THEME_ID = "t3-dark"; export function getTheme(id: string): Theme // fallback → DEFAULT_THEME_ID export function listThemes(): readonly Theme[] // display order ``` Implement with a `Map` for O(1) lookup. ### Step 2 — `packages/shared/package.json` Add after the `"./Net"` entry: ```json "./themes": { "types": "./src/themes.ts", "import": "./src/themes.ts" } ``` ### Step 3 — `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` (overhaul) Replace the current light/dark/system implementation with a multi-theme implementation. Import from `@t3tools/shared/themes`. **Storage key**: keep `"t3code:theme"`, but now stores a theme ID string. **Migration** from old format (executed inside `getStoredThemeId()`): - `"light"` → `"t3-light"` - `"dark"` or `"system"` → `"t3-dark"` (system users default to dark) - unknown string → `DEFAULT_THEME_ID` via `getTheme(raw).id` **`applyTheme(theme: Theme, suppressTransitions?: boolean)`** function: 1. Optionally add `.no-transitions` to `` (existing pattern, keep it) 2. Toggle `.dark` class via `classList.toggle("dark", theme.isDark)` — needed by `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` (`useTheme().resolvedTheme`) and Tailwind `dark:` variants 3. Set CSS custom properties on `document.documentElement` via `style.setProperty`: - `--background` ← `theme.colors.background` - `--foreground` ← `theme.colors.foreground` - `--primary` ← `theme.colors.accent` - `--ring` ← `theme.colors.accent` - `--t3-sidebar` ← `theme.colors.sidebar` (new var, for future component use) - `--t3-editor` ← `theme.colors.editor` - `--t3-terminal` ← `theme.colors.terminal` - `--t3-status-bar` ← `theme.colors.statusBar` - `--theme-font-family` ← `theme.fontFamily` - `--theme-font-size` ← `${theme.fontSize}px` 4. Optionally remove `.no-transitions` in a rAF (existing pattern) The remaining variables (`--card`, `--popover`, `--muted`, `--border`, etc.) are **inherited** from the CSS file's `:root` / `@variant dark` block, since the `.dark` class toggle already selects the right set. Only the variables listed above get inline style overrides, meaning `t3-midnight` benefits from dark-mode defaults + overrides its custom bg/fg/accent. **Module-level initialization** (keep existing pattern): ```typescript applyTheme(getTheme(getStoredThemeId())); // prevent flash on load ``` **`useSyncExternalStore` wiring** (same external-store pattern as today): - `subscribe`: register listener, listen to `window.addEventListener("storage", ...)` for cross-tab sync (no media-query listener needed since we dropped system mode) - `getSnapshot`: return current `Theme` object (memoized with identity-stable ref) **Returned API**: ```typescript export function useTheme() { // ... return { theme, // Theme object (id, name, colors, fontFamily, fontSize, isDark) setTheme, // (id: string) => void availableThemes, // listThemes() — stable reference, no memo needed resolvedTheme, // "light" | "dark" — derived from theme.isDark; backward-compat } as const; } ``` `resolvedTheme` keeps `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` working unchanged. ### Step 4 — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` (new file) A self-contained component that pulls from `useTheme()` and renders a 3-column grid of theme cards. Pattern: follow existing component style (Tailwind utilities, `cn()`, no external deps). Each card: - A mini preview `div` (`h-12`, `overflow-hidden`, `rounded-lg`) styled with inline `backgroundColor` from `theme.colors.background`: - Left strip (w-5): `theme.colors.sidebar` - Two faux text lines (bottom): `theme.colors.foreground` at low opacity - Accent dot (top-right): `theme.colors.accent` - Theme name (`text-xs font-medium`) + "Active" badge when selected (matching existing badge style from `_chat.settings.tsx`) - Three color swatch circles (`size-3 rounded-full`) for background / accent / foreground - Border highlight when selected: `border-primary/60 bg-primary/8` (matches existing radio button selected style) - `role="radio"` / `aria-checked` / `role="radiogroup"` on wrapper (match existing) ### Step 5 — `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` **Replace** the Appearance section's body (the `THEME_OPTIONS` constant, the `space-y-2 role="radiogroup"` div, and the "Active theme: …" paragraph) with: ```tsx import { ThemeSelector } from "../components/ThemeSelector"; // inside SettingsRouteView, destructure: const { theme } = useTheme(); // (resolvedTheme and old THEME_OPTIONS are no longer needed)

Active theme: {theme.name}

``` Remove `THEME_OPTIONS` constant and update the `useTheme` destructure to drop `resolvedTheme` (no longer used in this file). ### Step 6 — `apps/web/src/index.css` In the existing `body` block (line ~123), update `font-family` to use the CSS variable with the existing font stack as the inline fallback: ```css body { font-family: var( --theme-font-family, "DM Sans", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif ); } ``` Since all three built-in themes use DM Sans, this is a no-op at runtime until a user creates a custom theme — but it wires the fontFamily field into the browser. (Font-size: leave the body default font-size alone; `--theme-font-size` is set by `applyTheme` and available as a CSS variable for any component that needs it, but we don't globally override the base font size to avoid cascading layout breakage.) --- ## Existing Reuse & Compatibility Notes - **`DiffWorkerPoolProvider`** (`src/components/DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx`): consumes `useTheme().resolvedTheme` — unchanged because `resolvedTheme` is still returned. - **`cn()` utility** (`src/lib/utils.ts`): used in ThemeSelector, no change needed. - **External store pattern**: the overhaul of `useTheme.ts` keeps the same `useSyncExternalStore` + module-level store pattern as today. - **`"t3code:theme"` storage key**: reused, migration logic handles old values. - **`packages/shared` tsconfig**: includes `src/**`, so `themes.ts` is covered automatically; no tsconfig changes needed. --- ## Verification 1. **TypeCheck**: `bun typecheck` from repo root — must pass for all packages. 2. **Lint**: `bun lint` from repo root — must pass. 3. **Visual smoke test**: - Open the app; default should render as T3 Dark (no flash). - Navigate to Settings → Appearance; ThemeSelector should show 3 cards, T3 Dark active. - Click T3 Light: background flips to white, accent stays purple, `.dark` class removed from ``. - Click T3 Midnight: deep blue-tinted dark, different accent than T3 Dark. - Reload — selected theme persists (localStorage). - Open a second tab — switching theme in one tab should sync the other (via `storage` event listener). 4. **DiffWorkerPoolProvider**: open a diff panel; diff theme should switch with the app theme (`resolvedTheme` drives it). 5. **Settings page** still renders the Codex, Models, Responses, Keybindings, Safety sections unchanged.

Follow the same style as `git.ts`/`model.ts`: JSDoc, `readonly` properties, `as const satisfies`, exported pure functions. ```typescript export interface ThemeColors { readonly background: string; // main app background readonly foreground: string; // primary text readonly accent: string; // primary action color (buttons, focus rings) readonly sidebar: string; // sidebar panel background readonly editor: string; // editor pane background readonly terminal: string; // terminal background readonly statusBar: string; // status-bar background } export interface Theme { readonly id: string; readonly name: string; readonly colors: ThemeColors; readonly fontFamily: string; readonly fontSize: number; // base font size in px readonly isDark: boolean; // drives .dark class toggle for Tailwind } ``` Three built-in themes (colors chosen to match / diverge from existing CSS): - **`"t3-dark"`** (default): matches the existing dark-mode CSS variables (`oklch(0.14 0 0)` bg, `oklch(0.96 0 0)` fg, `oklch(0.588 0.217 264)` accent). - **`"t3-light"`**: matches the existing light-mode CSS variables (`#ffffff` bg, `oklch(0.271 0 0)` fg, `oklch(0.488 0.217 264)` accent). - **`"t3-midnight"`**: deep blue-tinted dark; distinct from t3-dark (`oklch(0.1 0.015 265)` bg, `oklch(0.93 0.01 265)` fg, `oklch(0.65 0.26 275)` accent). Export: ```typescript export const DEFAULT_THEME_ID = "t3-dark"; export function getTheme(id: string): Theme // fallback → DEFAULT_THEME_ID export function listThemes(): readonly Theme[] // display order ``` Implement with a `Map` for O(1) lookup. ### Step 2 — `packages/shared/package.json` Add after the `"./Net"` entry: ```json "./themes": { "types": "./src/themes.ts", "import": "./src/themes.ts" } ``` ### Step 3 — `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` (overhaul) Replace the current light/dark/system implementation with a multi-theme implementation. Import from `@t3tools/shared/themes`. **Storage key**: keep `"t3code:theme"`, but now stores a theme ID string. **Migration** from old format (executed inside `getStoredThemeId()`): - `"light"` → `"t3-light"` - `"dark"` or `"system"` → `"t3-dark"` (system users default to dark) - unknown string → `DEFAULT_THEME_ID` via `getTheme(raw).id` **`applyTheme(theme: Theme, suppressTransitions?: boolean)`** function: 1. Optionally add `.no-transitions` to `` (existing pattern, keep it) 2. Toggle `.dark` class via `classList.toggle("dark", theme.isDark)` — needed by `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` (`useTheme().resolvedTheme`) and Tailwind `dark:` variants 3. Set CSS custom properties on `document.documentElement` via `style.setProperty`: - `--background` ← `theme.colors.background` - `--foreground` ← `theme.colors.foreground` - `--primary` ← `theme.colors.accent` - `--ring` ← `theme.colors.accent` - `--t3-sidebar` ← `theme.colors.sidebar` (new var, for future component use) - `--t3-editor` ← `theme.colors.editor` - `--t3-terminal` ← `theme.colors.terminal` - `--t3-status-bar` ← `theme.colors.statusBar` - `--theme-font-family` ← `theme.fontFamily` - `--theme-font-size` ← `${theme.fontSize}px` 4. Optionally remove `.no-transitions` in a rAF (existing pattern) The remaining variables (`--card`, `--popover`, `--muted`, `--border`, etc.) are **inherited** from the CSS file's `:root` / `@variant dark` block, since the `.dark` class toggle already selects the right set. Only the variables listed above get inline style overrides, meaning `t3-midnight` benefits from dark-mode defaults + overrides its custom bg/fg/accent. **Module-level initialization** (keep existing pattern): ```typescript applyTheme(getTheme(getStoredThemeId())); // prevent flash on load ``` **`useSyncExternalStore` wiring** (same external-store pattern as today): - `subscribe`: register listener, listen to `window.addEventListener("storage", ...)` for cross-tab sync (no media-query listener needed since we dropped system mode) - `getSnapshot`: return current `Theme` object (memoized with identity-stable ref) **Returned API**: ```typescript export function useTheme() { // ... return { theme, // Theme object (id, name, colors, fontFamily, fontSize, isDark) setTheme, // (id: string) => void availableThemes, // listThemes() — stable reference, no memo needed resolvedTheme, // "light" | "dark" — derived from theme.isDark; backward-compat } as const; } ``` `resolvedTheme` keeps `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` working unchanged. ### Step 4 — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` (new file) A self-contained component that pulls from `useTheme()` and renders a 3-column grid of theme cards. Pattern: follow existing component style (Tailwind utilities, `cn()`, no external deps). Each card: - A mini preview `div` (`h-12`, `overflow-hidden`, `rounded-lg`) styled with inline `backgroundColor` from `theme.colors.background`: - Left strip (w-5): `theme.colors.sidebar` - Two faux text lines (bottom): `theme.colors.foreground` at low opacity - Accent dot (top-right): `theme.colors.accent` - Theme name (`text-xs font-medium`) + "Active" badge when selected (matching existing badge style from `_chat.settings.tsx`) - Three color swatch circles (`size-3 rounded-full`) for background / accent / foreground - Border highlight when selected: `border-primary/60 bg-primary/8` (matches existing radio button selected style) - `role="radio"` / `aria-checked` / `role="radiogroup"` on wrapper (match existing) ### Step 5 — `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` **Replace** the Appearance section's body (the `THEME_OPTIONS` constant, the `space-y-2 role="radiogroup"` div, and the "Active theme: …" paragraph) with: ```tsx import { ThemeSelector } from "../components/ThemeSelector"; // inside SettingsRouteView, destructure: const { theme } = useTheme(); // (resolvedTheme and old THEME_OPTIONS are no longer needed)

Active theme: {theme.name}

``` Remove `THEME_OPTIONS` constant and update the `useTheme` destructure to drop `resolvedTheme` (no longer used in this file). ### Step 6 — `apps/web/src/index.css` In the existing `body` block (line ~123), update `font-family` to use the CSS variable with the existing font stack as the inline fallback: ```css body { font-family: var( --theme-font-family, "DM Sans", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif ); } ``` Since all three built-in themes use DM Sans, this is a no-op at runtime until a user creates a custom theme — but it wires the fontFamily field into the browser. Here's the complete implementation plan:

---

## Custom Theme System — Plan

### What I found in the codebase

- **Existing `useTheme.ts`**: Stores `"light" | "dark" | "system"` in localStorage key `"t3code:theme"`, toggles `.dark` CSS class on `<html>`, and returns `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }`. Uses `useSyncExternalStore` — the same pattern I'll keep.
- **CSS architecture**: Tailwind v4 with CSS custom properties in `index.css`. The `:root` block defines all semantic variables; `@variant dark` overrides them when `.dark` is on `<html>`.
- **Settings page** (`_chat.settings.tsx`): Already has an "Appearance" section with a 3-option radio group for light/dark/system — this gets replaced by the new `ThemeSelector` component.
- **`DiffWorkerPoolProvider`**: Consumes `useTheme().resolvedTheme` — must stay backward-compatible.
- **`packages/shared`**: Uses explicit subpath exports in `package.json` (e.g. `"./model"`, `"./git"`). New `themes.ts` follows the same pattern.

---

### 6 files to change (no new dependencies, no build config changes)

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` | **CREATE** — Theme interface, 3 built-in themes, `getTheme()`, `listThemes()` |
| `packages/shared/package.json` | **MODIFY** — add `"./themes"` subpath export |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | **OVERHAUL** — multi-theme system, backward-compat API |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | **CREATE** — visual theme picker with color swatches |
| `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | **MODIFY** — replace Appearance radio group with `<ThemeSelector />` |
| `apps/web/src/index.css` | **MODIFY** — `font-family: var(--theme-font-family, "DM Sans", …)` on body |

### Key design decisions

1. **`Theme` interface** in `packages/shared` has `id`, `name`, `isDark`, `fontFamily`, `fontSize`, and `colors: { background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, statusBar }`. Pure data, no DOM deps.

2. **Three built-in themes**: `t3-dark` (default, matches existing dark CSS), `t3-light` (matches existing light CSS), `t3-midnight` (deep blue-tinted dark with indigo accent — distinct look).

3. **CSS application strategy**: `applyTheme()` in the web hook sets `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--ring` (and the new `--t3-sidebar`, `--t3-editor`, `--t3-terminal`, `--t3-status-bar`, font vars) via `style.setProperty` on `document.documentElement`. All other variables (`--card`, `--muted`, `--border`, etc.) are **inherited** from the existing CSS file via the `.dark` class toggle — so `t3-midnight` gets all standard dark defaults + only overrides what it customizes.

4. **Backward compat**: `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` (derived from `theme.isDark`) is still returned so `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` works unchanged.

5. **Storage migration**: Old `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, old `"dark"` or `"system"` → `"t3-dark"`.

6. **No system-preference mode**: Dropped in favour of explicit named themes (system detection was tied to the old 3-option model).

Export: ```typescript export const DEFAULT_THEME_ID = "t3-dark"; export function getTheme(id: string): Theme // fallback → DEFAULT_THEME_ID export function listThemes(): readonly Theme[] // display order ``` Implement with a `Map` for O(1) lookup. ### Step 2 — `packages/shared/package.json` Add after the `"./Net"` entry: ```json "./themes": { "types": "./src/themes.ts", "import": "./src/themes.ts" } ``` ### Step 3 — `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` (overhaul) Replace the current light/dark/system implementation with a multi-theme implementation. Import from `@t3tools/shared/themes`. **Storage key**: keep `"t3code:theme"`, but now stores a theme ID string. **Migration** from old format (executed inside `getStoredThemeId()`): - `"light"` → `"t3-light"` - `"dark"` or `"system"` → `"t3-dark"` (system users default to dark) - unknown string → `DEFAULT_THEME_ID` via `getTheme(raw).id` **`applyTheme(theme: Theme, suppressTransitions?: boolean)`** function: 1. Optionally add `.no-transitions` to `` (existing pattern, keep it) 2. Toggle `.dark` class via `classList.toggle("dark", theme.isDark)` — needed by `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` (`useTheme().resolvedTheme`) and Tailwind `dark:` variants 3. Set CSS custom properties on `document.documentElement` via `style.setProperty`: - `--background` ← `theme.colors.background` - `--foreground` ← `theme.colors.foreground` - `--primary` ← `theme.colors.accent` - `--ring` ← `theme.colors.accent` - `--t3-sidebar` ← `theme.colors.sidebar` (new var, for future component use) - `--t3-editor` ← `theme.colors.editor` - `--t3-terminal` ← `theme.colors.terminal` - `--t3-status-bar` ← `theme.colors.statusBar` - `--theme-font-family` ← `theme.fontFamily` - `--theme-font-size` ← `${theme.fontSize}px` 4. Optionally remove `.no-transitions` in a rAF (existing pattern) The remaining variables (`--card`, `--popover`, `--muted`, `--border`, etc.) are **inherited** from the CSS file's `:root` / `@variant dark` block, since the `.dark` class toggle already selects the right set. Only the variables listed above get inline style overrides, meaning `t3-midnight` benefits from dark-mode defaults + overrides its custom bg/fg/accent. **Module-level initialization** (keep existing pattern): ```typescript applyTheme(getTheme(getStoredThemeId())); // prevent flash on load ``` **`useSyncExternalStore` wiring** (same external-store pattern as today): - `subscribe`: register listener, listen to `window.addEventListener("storage", ...)` for cross-tab sync (no media-query listener needed since we dropped system mode) - `getSnapshot`: return current `Theme` object (memoized with identity-stable ref) **Returned API**: ```typescript export function useTheme() { // ... return { theme, // Theme object (id, name, colors, fontFamily, fontSize, isDark) setTheme, // (id: string) => void availableThemes, // listThemes() — stable reference, no memo needed resolvedTheme, // "light" | "dark" — derived from theme.isDark; backward-compat } as const; } ``` `resolvedTheme` keeps `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` working unchanged. ### Step 4 — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` (new file) A self-contained component that pulls from `useTheme()` and renders a 3-column grid of theme cards. Pattern: follow existing component style (Tailwind utilities, `cn()`, no external deps). Each card: - A mini preview `div` (`h-12`, `overflow-hidden`, `rounded-lg`) styled with inline `backgroundColor` from `theme.colors.background`: - Left strip (w-5): `theme.colors.sidebar` - Two faux text lines (bottom): `theme.colors.foreground` at low opacity - Accent dot (top-right): `theme.colors.accent` - Theme name (`text-xs font-medium`) + "Active" badge when selected (matching existing badge style from `_chat.settings.tsx`) - Three color swatch circles (`size-3 rounded-full`) for background / accent / foreground - Border highlight when selected: `border-primary/60 bg-primary/8` (matches existing radio button selected style) - `role="radio"` / `aria-checked` / `role="radiogroup"` on wrapper (match existing) ### Step 5 — `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` **Replace** the Appearance section's body (the `THEME_OPTIONS` constant, the `space-y-2 role="radiogroup"` div, and the "Active theme: …" paragraph) with: ```tsx import { ThemeSelector } from "../components/ThemeSelector"; // inside SettingsRouteView, destructure: const { theme } = useTheme(); // (resolvedTheme and old THEME_OPTIONS are no longer needed)

Active theme: {theme.name}

``` Remove `THEME_OPTIONS` constant and update the `useTheme` destructure to drop `resolvedTheme` (no longer used in this file). ### Step 6 — `apps/web/src/index.css` In the existing `body` block (line ~123), update `font-family` to use the CSS variable with the existing font stack as the inline fallback: ```css body { font-family: var( --theme-font-family, "DM Sans", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif ); } ``` Since all three built-in themes use DM Sans, this is a no-op at runtime until a user creates a custom theme — but it wires the fontFamily field into the browser. (Font-size: leave the body default font-size alone; `--theme-font-size` is set by `applyTheme` and available as a CSS variable for any component that needs it, but we don't globally override the base font size to avoid cascading layout breakage.) --- ## Existing Reuse & Compatibility Notes - **`DiffWorkerPoolProvider`** (`src/components/DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx`): consumes `useTheme().resolvedTheme` — unchanged because `resolvedTheme` is still returned. - **`cn()` utility** (`src/lib/utils.ts`): used in ThemeSelector, no change needed. - **External store pattern**: the overhaul of `useTheme.ts` keeps the same `useSyncExternalStore` + module-level store pattern as today. - **`"t3code:theme"` storage key**: reused, migration logic handles old values. - **`packages/shared` tsconfig**: includes `src/**`, so `themes.ts` is covered automatically; no tsconfig changes needed. --- ## Verification 1. **TypeCheck**: `bun typecheck` from repo root — must pass for all packages. 2. **Lint**: `bun lint` from repo root — must pass. 3. **Visual smoke test**: - Open the app; default should render as T3 Dark (no flash). - Navigate to Settings → Appearance; ThemeSelector should show 3 cards, T3 Dark active. - Click T3 Light: background flips to white, accent stays purple, `.dark` class removed from ``. - Click T3 Midnight: deep blue-tinted dark, different accent than T3 Dark. - Reload — selected theme persists (localStorage). - Open a second tab — switching theme in one tab should sync the other (via `storage` event listener). 4. **DiffWorkerPoolProvider**: open a diff panel; diff theme should switch with the app theme (`resolvedTheme` drives it). 5. **Settings page** still renders the Codex, Models, Responses, Keybindings, Safety sections unchanged.

1	Here's the complete implementation plan:
2	
3	---
4	
5	## Custom Theme System — Plan
6	
7	### What I found in the codebase
8	
9	- **Existing `useTheme.ts`**: Stores `"light" | "dark" | "system"` in localStorage key `"t3code:theme"`, toggles `.dark` CSS class on `<html>`, and returns `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }`. Uses `useSyncExternalStore` — the same pattern I'll keep.
10	- **CSS architecture**: Tailwind v4 with CSS custom properties in `index.css`. The `:root` block defines all semantic variables; `@variant dark` overrides them when `.dark` is on `<html>`.
11	- **Settings page** (`_chat.settings.tsx`): Already has an "Appearance" section with a 3-option radio group for light/dark/system — this gets replaced by the new `ThemeSelector` component.
12	- **`DiffWorkerPoolProvider`**: Consumes `useTheme().resolvedTheme` — must stay backward-compatible.
13	- **`packages/shared`**: Uses explicit subpath exports in `package.json` (e.g. `"./model"`, `"./git"`). New `themes.ts` follows the same pattern.
14	
15	---
16	
17	### 6 files to change (no new dependencies, no build config changes)
18	
19	| File | Action |
20	|---|---|
21	| `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` | **CREATE** — Theme interface, 3 built-in themes, `getTheme()`, `listThemes()` |
22	| `packages/shared/package.json` | **MODIFY** — add `"./themes"` subpath export |
23	| `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | **OVERHAUL** — multi-theme system, backward-compat API |
24	| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | **CREATE** — visual theme picker with color swatches |
25	| `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | **MODIFY** — replace Appearance radio group with `<ThemeSelector />` |
26	| `apps/web/src/index.css` | **MODIFY** — `font-family: var(--theme-font-family, "DM Sans", …)` on body |
27	
28	### Key design decisions
29	
30	1. **`Theme` interface** in `packages/shared` has `id`, `name`, `isDark`, `fontFamily`, `fontSize`, and `colors: { background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, statusBar }`. Pure data, no DOM deps.
31	
32	2. **Three built-in themes**: `t3-dark` (default, matches existing dark CSS), `t3-light` (matches existing light CSS), `t3-midnight` (deep blue-tinted dark with indigo accent — distinct look).
33	
34	3. **CSS application strategy**: `applyTheme()` in the web hook sets `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--ring` (and the new `--t3-sidebar`, `--t3-editor`, `--t3-terminal`, `--t3-status-bar`, font vars) via `style.setProperty` on `document.documentElement`. All other variables (`--card`, `--muted`, `--border`, etc.) are **inherited** from the existing CSS file via the `.dark` class toggle — so `t3-midnight` gets all standard dark defaults + only overrides what it customizes.
35	
36	4. **Backward compat**: `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` (derived from `theme.isDark`) is still returned so `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` works unchanged.
37	
38	5. **Storage migration**: Old `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, old `"dark"` or `"system"` → `"t3-dark"`.
39	
40	6. **No system-preference mode**: Dropped in favour of explicit named themes (system detection was tied to the old 3-option model).

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report. --- ## T3 Code Monorepo: Complete Codebase Exploration Report I've thoroughly explored the T3 Code monorepo. Here's everything you need to understand the structure and implement the theme system as outlined in PLAN.md. ### 1. PLAN.md Summary The PLAN.md file outlines a **custom theme system** implementation requiring **6 file changes**: - Create `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` - Theme interface, 3 built-in themes, helper functions - Modify `packages/shared/package.json` - Add `"./themes"` subpath export - Overhaul `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` - Multi-theme system with backward-compat API - Create `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` - Visual theme picker with color swatches - Modify `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` - Replace radio group with ThemeSelector - Modify `apps/web/src/index.css` - Add font-family CSS variable support **Key Design Points:** - Theme interface includes: `id`, `name`, `isDark`, `fontFamily`, `fontSize`, `colors` (background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, statusBar) - Three built-in themes: `t3-dark` (default), `t3-light`, `t3-midnight` (deep blue-tinted) - CSS strategy: Apply overrides via `setProperty` on `document.documentElement`, inherit standard variables from `.dark` class - Backward compat: `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` still returned for `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` - Storage migration: old `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, old `"dark"`/`"system"` → `"t3-dark"` --- ### 2. Monorepo Structure **Root location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775327714662-tmimjp` **High-level structure:** ``` root/ ├── apps/ │ ├── web/ (Vite + React SPA) │ ├── desktop/ │ ├── server/ │ ├── marketing/ ├── packages/ │ ├── shared/ (Shared utilities, exported via subpaths) │ ├── contracts/ (TypeScript types/interfaces) ├── scripts/ (Build/dev utilities) ├── package.json (Monorepo root with pnpm workspaces) ├── PLAN.md (Implementation plan) ``` **Monorepo Configuration (package.json):** - Package manager: `bun@1.3.9` - Workspaces: `apps/*` and `packages/*` - Uses Turbo for task orchestration - No major build config changes needed (Tailwind v4 already in place) --- ### 3. apps/web/src Structure - Complete Overview **Key files by category:** **Entry Points:** - `/apps/web/src/main.tsx` - Entry point, imports index.css and router, renders RouterProvider - `/apps/web/src/router.ts` - Creates TanStack router with QueryClientProvider and StoreProvider - `/apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx` - Root route component with ToastProvider, DiffWorkerPoolProvider context **Theme/CSS:** - `/apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` - **Current hook** using `useSyncExternalStore` pattern, stores `"light" | "dark" | "system"` in localStorage key `"t3code:theme"`, toggles `.dark` class on `` - `/apps/web/src/index.css` - **Tailwind v4** with `@import "tailwindcss"`, `:root` defines all semantic variables, `@variant dark` overrides for dark mode, scrollbar styling, chat markdown styles **Settings/UI:** - `/apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` - Settings page with Appearance section (currently 3-option radio group: System/Light/Dark). This is where `ThemeSelector` will replace the radio group. - `/apps/web/src/components/DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx` - **Uses `useTheme().resolvedTheme`** to sync diff theme with app theme. Must remain backward-compatible. **Route Structure (TanStack Router):** - `/apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx` - Root layout - `/apps/web/src/routes/_chat.tsx` - Chat layout - `/apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` - Settings page - `/apps/web/src/routes/_chat.$threadId.tsx` - Thread view - `/apps/web/src/routes/_chat.index.tsx` - Chat home **UI Components (apps/web/src/components/ui/):** - Standard shadcn-style components: button, input, select, radio-group, switch, badge, card, dialog, etc. - All use CSS class utilities (Tailwind) **Styling Details:** - Tailwind v4 with CSS custom properties (aka CSS variables) - Light theme: `--background: var(--color-white)`, `--foreground: var(--color-neutral-800)`, primary: `oklch(0.488 0.217 264)` (indigo-ish) - Dark theme: `--background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-neutral-950) 95%, var(--color-white))`, `--foreground: var(--color-neutral-100)`, primary: `oklch(0.588 0.217 264)` (lighter indigo) - Scrollbar: 6px width, themed with rgba transparency --- ### 4. packages/shared/src Structure **Current Files:** 1. `model.ts` - Model slug normalization, resolver functions, option getters 2. `git.ts` - Git branch name sanitization, feature branch resolution 3. `logging.ts` - Logging utilities 4. `shell.ts` - Shell command utilities 5. `Net.ts` - Network utilities **Export Pattern (package.json):** ```json "exports": { "./model": { "types": "./src/model.ts", "import": "./src/model.ts" }, "./git": { "types": "./src/git.ts", "import": "./src/git.ts" }, "./logging": { "types": "./src/logging.ts", "import": "./src/logging.ts" }, "./shell": { "types": "./src/shell.ts", "import": "./src/shell.ts" }, "./Net": { "types": "./src/Net.ts", "import": "./src/Net.ts" } } ``` **New subpath to add:** ```json "./themes": { "types": "./src/themes.ts", "import": "./src/themes.ts" } ``` This allows imports like: ```typescript import { getTheme, listThemes, type Theme } from "@t3tools/shared/themes" ``` --- ### 5. Key Import Conventions & Path Aliases **apps/web tsconfig.json:** - Path alias: `"~/*": ["./src/*"]` - allows `import { Button } from "~/components/ui/button"` - JSX: `"jsx": "react-jsx"` (automatic JSX transform) - Extends `../../tsconfig.base.json` **packages/shared tsconfig.json:** - Simple inheritance from base, no custom paths - Pure ESM, exports via subpaths in package.json **Import conventions used in apps/web:** ```typescript // Absolute imports from monorepo packages import { useTheme } from "../hooks/useTheme" import { getRouter } from "./router" import { DiffWorkerPoolProvider } from "../components/DiffWorkerPoolProvider" // From @t3tools packages import { type ProviderKind } from "@t3tools/contracts" import { getModelOptions } from "@t3tools/shared/model" // From node_modules import { useCallback } from "react" import { createFileRoute } from "@tanstack/react-router" ``` --- ### 6. Current useTheme.ts Hook - Detailed Breakdown **File:** `/apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` The hook uses the **External Store Pattern** with `useSyncExternalStore`: - Global `listeners` array and `lastSnapshot` for subscription management - `STORAGE_KEY = "t3code:theme"` stores `"light" | "dark" | "system"` - `applyTheme(theme)` function: - Adds/removes `.dark` class on `` - Suppresses transitions during toggle (adds `no-transitions` class, forces reflow, removes after RAF) - System preference detection via `window.matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)")` - Syncs across tabs via `storage` event listener - Returns `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }` where `resolvedTheme` is computed `"light" | "dark"` **Critical for backward compatibility:** - `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` consumes `useTheme().resolvedTheme` - Must keep returning `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` derived from `theme.isDark` --- ### 7. CSS Architecture - index.css **File:** `/apps/web/src/index.css` **Structure:** 1. **Tailwind import:** `@import "tailwindcss"` 2. **Custom variant:** `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *))` 3. **Theme definitions** in `:root` block with `@variant dark` overrides: - Radius: `--radius: 0.625rem` (with derived sizes: `--radius-sm`, `--radius-md`, etc.) - Light theme colors: primary `oklch(0.488 0.217 264)`, background white, foreground neutral-800 - Dark theme colors: primary lighter `oklch(0.588 0.217 264)`, background nearly black, foreground neutral-100 - All colors use CSS custom properties: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--card`, `--primary`, `--border`, `--input`, `--ring`, etc. 4. **Font family:** Hard-coded on `body`, will need CSS variable support 5. **Layout:** Full viewport sizing, no scrollbars 6. **Chat markdown:** Custom styles for `.chat-markdown` class 7. **Diff panel:** Themed backgrounds for `.diff-panel-viewport` **Key observation:** All component colors inherit from `:root` CSS variables. To support themes, we set CSS variables at runtime via `document.documentElement.style.setProperty()`. --- ### 8. Settings Page Structure **File:** `/apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` **Current Appearance Section (lines 202-243):** ```typescript

Appearance

Choose how T3 Code handles light and dark mode.

{THEME_OPTIONS.map((option) => (

Active theme: {resolvedTheme}

``` **Components used:** - `useTheme()` hook for `theme`, `setTheme`, `resolvedTheme` - Custom radio group UI (styled buttons, not using `` component) - Will be replaced by `` component **Other settings sections:** - Codex App Server (binary path, home path) - Models (custom model slugs) - Responses (stream assistant messages toggle) - Keybindings (open config file) - Safety (confirm thread deletion) --- ### 9. DiffWorkerPoolProvider Dependency **File:** `/apps/web/src/components/DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx` **Critical usage:** ```typescript export function DiffWorkerPoolProvider({ children }: { children?: ReactNode }) { const { resolvedTheme } = useTheme(); // Must get "light" or "dark" const diffThemeName = resolveDiffThemeName(resolvedTheme); // ... passes diffThemeName to worker pool } ``` **Constraint:** New `useTheme()` must continue returning `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` derived from the new theme's `isDark` property. --- ### 10. File Paths Summary (Absolute) **Core Implementation Files:** - `/apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` - Hook to overhaul - `/apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` - Settings page to modify - `/apps/web/src/index.css` - CSS to modify (add font-family variable) - `/apps/web/src/components/DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx` - Uses hook (must remain compatible) - `/apps/web/package.json` - Already has correct deps - `/apps/web/tsconfig.json` - Already correct **Shared Package Files:** - `/packages/shared/src/themes.ts` - Create new - `/packages/shared/package.json` - Modify exports **Import Entry Points:** - `/apps/web/src/main.tsx` - Entry, imports index.css - `/apps/web/src/router.ts` - Router setup --- ### 11. Key Technical Insights 1. **Monorepo Setup:** - Using `bun` as package manager (modern alternative to npm/yarn) - TanStack Router for routing (file-based via TurboPack plugin) - TanStack React Query for async state - Zustand for local stores 2. **Theme Application Strategy (from PLAN):** - Override CSS variables via `document.documentElement.style.setProperty()` - Keep `.dark` class toggle for backward compatibility and inherited defaults - Only override the specific colors each theme customizes 3. **React Patterns:** - `useSyncExternalStore` for external storage (localStorage) - File-based routing with TanStack Router - Context providers for global state (Toast, QueryClient, Store) 4. **No Breaking Changes Required:** - No dependency updates needed - No build config changes (Tailwind v4 already configured) - Backward compatibility via `resolvedTheme` return value 5. **CSS Variable Naming:** - Tailwind-mapped: `--color-background`, `--color-foreground`, `--color-primary`, etc. - Theme-specific: `--t3-sidebar`, `--t3-editor`, `--t3-terminal`, `--t3-status-bar` - Font vars: `--theme-font-family`, `--theme-font-size` --- This exploration provides everything needed to implement the theme system. The codebase is well-organized, uses modern React patterns, and has clear extension points for the theme system without breaking existing functionality.

Here's the complete implementation plan:

---

## Custom Theme System — Plan

### What I found in the codebase

- **Existing `useTheme.ts`**: Stores `"light" | "dark" | "system"` in localStorage key `"t3code:theme"`, toggles `.dark` CSS class on `<html>`, and returns `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }`. Uses `useSyncExternalStore` — the same pattern I'll keep.
- **CSS architecture**: Tailwind v4 with CSS custom properties in `index.css`. The `:root` block defines all semantic variables; `@variant dark` overrides them when `.dark` is on `<html>`.
- **Settings page** (`_chat.settings.tsx`): Already has an "Appearance" section with a 3-option radio group for light/dark/system — this gets replaced by the new `ThemeSelector` component.
- **`DiffWorkerPoolProvider`**: Consumes `useTheme().resolvedTheme` — must stay backward-compatible.
- **`packages/shared`**: Uses explicit subpath exports in `package.json` (e.g. `"./model"`, `"./git"`). New `themes.ts` follows the same pattern.

---

### 6 files to change (no new dependencies, no build config changes)

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` | **CREATE** — Theme interface, 3 built-in themes, `getTheme()`, `listThemes()` |
| `packages/shared/package.json` | **MODIFY** — add `"./themes"` subpath export |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | **OVERHAUL** — multi-theme system, backward-compat API |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | **CREATE** — visual theme picker with color swatches |
| `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | **MODIFY** — replace Appearance radio group with `<ThemeSelector />` |
| `apps/web/src/index.css` | **MODIFY** — `font-family: var(--theme-font-family, "DM Sans", …)` on body |

### Key design decisions

1. **`Theme` interface** in `packages/shared` has `id`, `name`, `isDark`, `fontFamily`, `fontSize`, and `colors: { background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, statusBar }`. Pure data, no DOM deps.

2. **Three built-in themes**: `t3-dark` (default, matches existing dark CSS), `t3-light` (matches existing light CSS), `t3-midnight` (deep blue-tinted dark with indigo accent — distinct look).

3. **CSS application strategy**: `applyTheme()` in the web hook sets `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--ring` (and the new `--t3-sidebar`, `--t3-editor`, `--t3-terminal`, `--t3-status-bar`, font vars) via `style.setProperty` on `document.documentElement`. All other variables (`--card`, `--muted`, `--border`, etc.) are **inherited** from the existing CSS file via the `.dark` class toggle — so `t3-midnight` gets all standard dark defaults + only overrides what it customizes.

4. **Backward compat**: `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` (derived from `theme.isDark`) is still returned so `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` works unchanged.

5. **Storage migration**: Old `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, old `"dark"` or `"system"` → `"t3-dark"`.

6. **No system-preference mode**: Dropped in favour of explicit named themes (system detection was tied to the old 3-option model).
